keyRules: Up to 120 m above the ground (EASA Open Category)
Register as a UAS operator with the RDW (€23 one-time fee + €13/year renewal fee) if the drone weighs 250 g or more or is equipped with a camera
A1/A3 online training is mandatory — minimum age 16
The 14-km CTR around Schiphol covers most of Amsterdam—see the official airspace map
Always maintain visual line of sight (VLOS) of the drone
Never fly over people, crowds, or public events
A minimum distance of 3 km from small airports, with a larger buffer zone around Schiphol
Night flying is prohibited without specific permission
Nature Areas (Natura 2000): Restrictions During the Breeding Season
Densely populated urban areas in the Randstad: check local municipal regulations
importantNotes: The 14-km CTR around Schiphol effectively bans drones from flying over most of Amsterdam
Registration through the RDW costs a one-time fee of €23 plus an annual renewal fee of €13 (effective 2025) — valid in all EU/EASA countries
Fines of up to €11,250 for violations
Military zones (Leeuwarden, Eindhoven, Volkel): absolute flight ban
The GDPR applies when creating images that depict identifiable individuals

fields.disclaimerText: Always check the official sources from ILT and RDW and the airspace map from IenW before you fly. This app is a tool—not a legal source. The Netherlands follows EASA regulations.
